mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and economic expansion, which typically supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significantly elevated unemployment rate, particularly after a sharp increase, signals deep economic distress and reduced consumer spending, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE is above 10%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales growth and low federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, which together drive corporate revenue and lead to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods with strong retail sales growth and low FEDFUNDS do not show significantly higher S&P 500 20-day returns compared to other periods (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly rising inflation, as measured by CPI, combined with an increasing federal funds rate, signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing price pressures, which typically dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If during periods where both CPI and FEDFUNDS are rising significantly, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in retail sales after a period of decline indicates renewed consumer confidence and spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month change > 5.0% after a prior 3-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales sharply declined in early 2020 due to COVID-19
failure_condition: If a 5% monthly increase in RSAFS following a decline does not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic contraction, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production sharply declined in early 2020 due to COVID-19
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 1% on average over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates growing consumer confidence and investment in real estate, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic recovery and subsequently driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts declined in early 2020 due to COVID-19
failure_condition: If a 10% average increase in HOUST over 3 months does not correlate with positive S&P 500 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is low and stable, and the Federal Reserve maintains a low federal funds rate, it signals an environment conducive to economic growth and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both CPIAUCSL growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.
